creator: jlduran@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Marek Zarychta from comment #17)
I think it is likely we'll rename our occurrences of BLACKLIST_BAD_USER to BLACKLIST_AUTH_FAIL. This should almost match the behavior you originally submitted, without the runtime "-U".

Also, there is one probe in crypto/openssh/auth-pam.c that logs BLACKLIST_NOTIFY(NULL, BLACKLIST_BAD_USER, sshpam_authctxt->user), the first parameter (NULL) does nothing at the moment.

I plan on putting a public SSH server to the test, that is more closely aligned with NetBSD's implementation (WIP here: https://github.com/jlduran/freebsd-src/pull/105), and addresses some (most?) of the issues described in comment #1.

There is still at least one SSH preauth miss that has been discovered (for both implementations), but the check for that one will be added later.
